title: Handling Duplicate Numbers in Binary Search

statement.markdown: Given a sorted array of integers and a target element, implement a binary search algorithm to find the target element. If the target element appears multiple times, return the first occurrence. If the target element is not found, return -1.
statement.inputFormat:
Input will be in the following format: [1st line: count of elements, 2nd line: elements separated by space]
Example:
3
1 5 3
statement.outputFormat: Return the index of the target element. If not found, return -1.
statement.notes: You can assume the array is sorted in ascending order.

examples.explanation: Target element 6 is not found in the array.

hints.text: When the target is found, update the result to the first occurrence.
When the target is not found, consider edge cases like an empty array or a single-element array.

editorial.title: Binary Search with Handling Duplicate Numbers
editorial.intuition: Use a binary search approach with a minor modification to handle duplicate numbers.
editorial.bruteForce: Iterate through the array from left to right or vice versa.
editorial.optimalSolution: Perform a binary search. If the target is found, check if it's the first occurrence.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The modified binary search correctly handles duplicate numbers and finds the first occurrence.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example to handle duplicate numbers in a binary search algorithm.

functionSignature.java: public boolean binarySearch(int[] nums, int target);
functionSignature.python: def binary_search(self, nums: list[int], target: int) -> bool:
